key_insight: MISTnetX effectively bridges molecular dynamics and finite-element models for shock-to-detonation predictions in energetic materials.
engineering_relevance: This approach addresses scale separation challenges in multiscale modeling, enhancing predictive accuracy for energetic materials.
method: Utilizes a convolutional deep neural network (MISTnetX) trained on MD simulations.
Captures interactions between shocks and complex microstructures.
Provides sub-grid information for finite-element simulations.
evidence_signal: The model enables parameter-free predictions for the full run-to-detonation transition in a synthetic nanostructured RDX composite.
watch_item: Potential limitations in the model's applicability to different materials or shock conditions.
paper_title: Microstructure-Aware Deep Learning Bridges Atomistics to Macroscale for Shock-to-Detonation Prediction
